1.1. Purpose
This Test Plan defines the documented testing strategy for System Testing (ST) and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) to ensure the Learning Management System (LMS), Version 1.0 is tested with sufficient rigor to demonstrate the system is fi t for intended use and operates correctly and consistently from a regulatory and business perspective throughout the validation lifecycle.
This Test Plan is a Test Planning deliverable and is intended to meet the Test Planning requirements defined in

QMS-WI-123, Computer System Validation (CSV) - Testing , including definition of:
- testing approach and scope,
- test environment (hardware/software), test data, and tools,
- roles and responsibilities,
- training required for test execution,
- traceability expectations, and
- overall acceptance criteria.
This Test Plan also supports the CSV process requirement that validation efforts result in recorded evidence of activities completed , and acknowledges that deliverables may be combined based on risk provided required content and approvals are addressed.
Configuration Item Number :, Learning Management System (LMS) = CI-LMS-001. Software Version:, Learning Management System (LMS) = 1.0 (mock). Vendor Version (if applicable):, Learning Management System (LMS) = 2026.1 (mock). Testing Plan: (Check as applicable), Learning Management System (LMS) = ☐ System Test (ST) Plan ☐ User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan ☒ Combined System and User Acceptance Test Plan ☐ Other

1.2. Scope
The scope of testing defined in this Test Plan includes ST and UAT activities required to demonstrate that the LMS meets its approved requirements and supports its intended use. Testing scope is driven by the validation strategy and the Requirements Risk Rating (RRR) contained within the approved Requirements Specification (RS).

1.2.1. In Scope
The following functional areas and control objectives are in scope for LMS Version 1.0 testing:
#, 1 = Functions/Enhancements/Bug Fixes. #, 2 = Type of Testing

1.2.1. In Scope

1, 1 = Core Training Management Business Processes (Intended Use) • Course and curriculum creation/maintenance (admin functions) • Assignment and scheduling of training to learners • Learner access to assigned training and completion capture • Training completion tracking, status management, and history retrieval. 1, 2 = ☐ ST ☒ UAT. 2, 1 = Electronic Records and Data Integrity Controls • Creation, modification, and retention of electronic training records • Verification that record outputs (e.g., completion history) are accurate and complete • Controls that prevent unauthorized record modification (role enforcement) • Verification of record protection mechanisms appropriate to system roles. 2, 2 = ☒ ST ☐ UAT. #, 1 = Functions/Enhancements/Bug Fixes. #, 2 = Type of Testing. 3, 1 = Security &Access Controls • User authentication mechanism for internal users • Role-based access control (admin vs learner vs reporting roles) • Verification of restricted access for privileged functions (configuration/reporting/admin). 3, 2 = ☐ ST ☒ UAT. 4, 1 = Audit Trail (where implemented for GxP-relevant records) • Verification that audit trail captures required events for in- scope records • Verification that audit trail entries are

1.2.1. In Scope
 attributable and retrievable (as applicable to LMS configuration). 4, 2 = ☒ ST ☐ UAT. , 1 = Reports &Outputs • Execution and verification of GxP-relevant training reports (PDF/CSV outputs) • Verification of report content accuracy for a defined set of training scenarios. , 2 = ☒ ST ☐ UAT

1.2.2.Out of Scope
The following items are out of scope for this Test Plan and will not be tested under this deliverable:

A. Category 1 infrastructure qualification activities
- Category 1 (Infrastructure) activities are out of scope of the CSV processes defined by the WI/SOP.
- B. Disaster Recovery / Business Continuity testing
- Disaster recovery activities are controlled under separate IT procedures and are not included within this Test Plan scope.

C. Performance / Load / Stress testing
- Performance testing is only included if performance requirements are defined; no such requirements are included in this PoC scope, therefore performance/load/stress testing is excluded.

D. Vendor internal regression testing
- Vendor internal development/regression testing is not documented within this LMS Test Plan; any reliance on vendor testing or documentation must be defined in the relevant planning deliverable for hosted solutions.

2.0 Testing Approach
The creation, review, pre-execution approval and post-execution approval of test scripts will be completed in compliance with QMS-WI-2A-2-4, Computer System Validation (CSV) - Testing .
Will testing be completed in phases; any overlap between ST and UAT? Specifically, will the UAT begin before ST is completed? ☒ Yes ☐ No
If yes, specify/justify :
System Testing (ST) and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) will be executed in phases, with limited overlap permitted.
As a general rule, ST must be completed prior to the start of UAT for the same module or functionality. However, UAT may begin for a specific module once ST has been successfully completed for that module, even if ST activities for other independent modules are still ongoing.
This phased approach allows efficient execution while ensuring that no UAT is performed on functionality that has not completed System Testing. All overlaps are controlled, documented, and traceable.
Is there regression testing of functions that are not being changed? ☒ Explain/justify :
- [ ] Yes
- [ ] ☐ No

2.0 Testing Approach
Regression testing will be performed on selected LMS functions that are not directly modified but may be indirectly impacted by configuration changes or enhancements introduced in this release.
Regression testing scope is determined based on risk and includes verification of critical training workflows, user access controls, and reporting functions to ensure no unintended impact to previously validated functionality.
How will the test scripts be executed?
- [x] ☒ Test scripts will be executed using Application Lifecycle Management (ALM)
- [ ] ☐ Test scripts will be executed on paper
- [ ] ☐ Other
Are changes impacting any business partners who use the system?
If yes, specify/justify : N/A

2.1 System Testing Approach
System Testing is performed to verify that the LMS, as configured and integrated, meets the approved specification set used to drive ST (e.g., Requirements Specification / Functional Requirements) and that key workflows operate as designed under controlled conditions.
System testing will be performed by Team A after the approval of IQ scripts in the test environment. System testing will be performed by Team A qafter the completion of IQ in the test environment.

Additional Notes :
No separate ST Summary Report will be generated. System Testing results will be documented within the Combined ST/UAT Summary Report. An ST Closure memo is not required as ST completion is formally documented and approved within the combined summary deliverable.

2.2 User Acceptance Testing Approach
User Acceptance Testing is performed to confirm that the LMS meets business/user requirements (intended use) when executed by representative users following defined workflows and acceptance criteria.

- [x] ☒ No

: N/A
What deliverables will be completed to summarize the UAT process?
- [ ] ☐ UAT Summary Report
- [x] ☒ Combined ST/UAT Summary Report

Additional Notes :
User Acceptance Testing results will be summarized within the Combined ST/UAT Summary Report. No standalone UAT Summary Report will be generated for this testing effort.

3.1 Test Environment
Testing will be performed in Dedicated LMS Test Environment which closely reflects the planned production environment.
No separate approved system architecture or design document exists that lists the test environment information; therefore, the test environment details are documented below.
Testing will be performed in the LMS-TEST environment. The table below describes the hardware and software requirements for the test environment:
Web, Server Name = LMS-WEB-TEST. Web, Operating System = Linux (RHEL 8.x). Web, Software and Version = LMS Web UI v1.0 (HTTPS enabled). Application, Server Name = LMS-APP-TEST. Application, Operating System = Linux (RHEL 8.x). Application, Software and Version = LMS Application Services v1.0. Database, Server Name = LMS-DB-TEST. Database, Operating System = Linux (RHEL 8.x). Database, Software and Version = PostgreSQL 13.x

3.2 Test Data
Test data required for this testing effort will be synthetic and mock data only , created specifically to support System Testing (ST) and User Acceptance Testing (UAT).
No production data will be copied or migrated from the production environment into the test environment.
Are a complete or subset of production data being copied from the system's production environment?
- [ ] ☐ Yes
- [x] ☒ No
Will data be migrated from another system into the testing environment?
- [ ] ☐ Yes
- [x] ☒ No
If yes, specify/ justify : N/A
Will the data be manually created during/prior to test execution?
- [x] ☒ Yes
- [ ] ☐ No
Is there another mechanism that is used to load data into the system for this testing effort?
- [ ] ☐ Yes
- [x] ☒ No
If yes specify/ justify
: N/A

Additional Notes :
- Test data will be manually created and configured by the testing team before test execution.
- Test data includes synthetic learner accounts, administrator accounts, sample courses, curricula, training assignments, completion records, and report outputs.
- Test data loading is completed prior to execution of test scripts and verified as part of test preparation activities.
- No production data is used for this testing effort.
- Test data does not contain Personal Identifiable Information (PII).

5.0 Test Defect Management
The purpose of this section is to summarize the defect management approach in the event defects are found during the testing phase.
Test defects will be managed in compliance with the applicable CSV Testing procedures. Defects will be documented using a controlled electronic system.

- [x] ☒ ALM
- [ ] ☐ Paper
- [ ] ☐ Other.  Specify:

- [ ] ☐ Service Now
- [ ] ☐ Infinity
- [ ] ☐ Other.  Specify.

6.0 Traceability Matrix
How is the traceability matrix being managed?
- [ ] ☐ Project Specific Document
- [ ] ☐ Living Document
- [x] ☒ Combination Project Specific and Living Document

Additional Notes :
- Traceability will link requirements to test scripts and test execution results using the Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) tool.
- The project-specific traceability matrix will be reviewed and verified upon completion of testing.
- Final traceability confirmation will be documented as part of the Test Summary Report.
- Any requirement or defect not directly traceable to a test script will include documented justification or alternative control.

7.0 Training Plan
The training items listed below are required for members of the project team based on their respective roles and responsibilities in support of testing activities for Learning Management System (LMS) Version 1.0 .
All personnel involved in System Testing (ST) and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) must be appropriately trained prior to execution of testing activities. Training ensures that testing is performed in a controlled manner and that testing records generated are complete, accurate, and compliant with applicable documentation standards.

Application Lifecycle Management (ALM)
- Training on the use of the Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) tool for:
- o Test script execution
- o Capture of objective evidence (screenshots, logs)
- o Defect logging and tracking
- o Traceability between requirements, test scripts, and execution results
- Review of applicable ALM job aids and quick reference guides prior to test execution

8.0 Test Deliverables
The following are the expected deliverables for this test effort:
Test Plan, Planned Storage Location = LMS Validation Document Repository. Approved, Executed test scripts with supporting evidence, where appropriate, Planned Storage Location = Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) tool. Traceability Matrix (required only for validated systems, recommended for non validated systems), Planned Storage Location = Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) tool. Test Summary Report, including information regarding the resolution of all test defects, Planned Storage Location = LMS Validation Document Repository

9.0 Test Acceptance Criteria
- Test scripts and corresponding objective evidence will be properly documented.
- Testing illustrates that the system meets the intended use as defined in the requirements.
- Test defects will be documented and dispositioned.

10.0 Roles and Responsibilities
The roles and responsibilities for this testing effort are defined in the applicable Solution Lifecycle Controls (SLC) procedures (where applicable) and the CSV Testing work instruction. The reviewers and approvers identified below are assigned based on their roles and responsibilities for testing activities associated with Learning Management System (LMS) Version 1.0 .
Roles may be performed by the same individual where permitted; however, responsibilities related to quality oversight and approval remain independent from test execution activities.

· Test Lead
Responsible for planning, coordinating, and managing System Testing (ST) and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) activities. The Test Lead ensures test scripts are prepared, executed, retested as required, and that testing results are accurately documented.

· Validation Lead
Responsible for ensuring that testing activities are executed in accordance with CSV requirements and that testing deliverables align with the overall validation strategy. The Validation Lead reviews and approves testing deliverables as required.

· Quality Assurance
Responsible for reviewing and approving testing deliverables to ensure compliance with regulatory and quality requirements. Quality Assurance provides independent oversight of the testing process.

· Business Owner
Responsible for confirming that the LMS meets intended business use through review and approval of User Acceptance Testing results. The Business Owner provides acceptance of the system from a business process perspective.

· Tester
Responsible for executing approved test scripts, documenting results, capturing objective evidence, and identifying defects encountered during testing.

11.0 References
QMS-SOP-1, Title = Computer System Validation (CSV). QMS-SOP-2, Title = GxP Good Documentation Practices
